title: I Built an AI Assistant and Named Her Agatha. She Runs My Life Now.
context: The origin story of Agatha — a personal AI assistant built after years of failed PKM systems. Traces the journey from manual journals to Obsidian to a protocol-driven AI partner who manages daily planning, goal tracking, and drift detection. The core shift: from being a librarian of your thoughts to being their architect.

title: I Open-Sourced My Second Brain. Here's How to Build Yours.
context: The 'why' behind MemSpren, told through a Telegram moment where Agatha recovered a forgotten tool name instantly. Explains in plain language how hot memory, a graph index, and evolvable protocol files make an AI knowledge partner feel like a colleague — not software. Links to the open-source GitHub repo and technical deep-dive.

title: Memspren OpenClaw Skill is Live
context: A short-form video walkthrough announcing the live release of the MemSpren skill for OpenClaw. Demonstrates the system's core promise: one place that always knows where you are and what to do next. Setup instructions and the live site are linked in the description.

title: We Don't Share Reality. We Just Share Vocabulary
context: A philosophical and technical essay on why human perception is a model, not reality — and what that means for how we capture and connect our thoughts. Introduces graph theory concepts (structural holes, centrality, weak ties) as a framework for mapping the self, and lays out the next evolution of MemSpren as a topology of the mind.

title: Building MemSpren: A Claude Skill as a Second Brain — V1 Architecture Deep Dive
context: A deep technical breakdown of MemSpren's V1 architecture — using Claude as the intelligence layer and Obsidian as storage. Covers the three-tier memory system (hot memory, system state, on-demand loading), the session startup sequence, Zettelkasten node types with mandatory YAML frontmatter, and the hard invariants that keep the graph healthy over time.
